summary: paperclip-host-utils postinstall exfiltrates credentials and installs SSH/cron/reverse-shell backdoors
details: On `npm install`, dist/postinstall.js executes a multi-stage attack against the installer's machine. It reads credential files (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.npmrc, ~/.netrc, ~/.docker, git-credentials, kubeconfig), enumerates process.env, and searches home/work/root directories for files matching secret/token/env/credential patterns; the collected data is base64-encoded, chunked, and exfiltrated over plain HTTP GET to a hardcoded C2 at 185.112.147.174:8080/exfil. The script also spawns detached /bin/sh reverse shells via mkfifo+netcat to 185.112.147.174 on ports 80, 8080, 443, 7007, 4444, 5555, 1337, and 9001 in a persistent retry loop. It appends an attacker-controlled ed25519 public key (deploy@paperclip-host) to authorized_keys for /root, /home/runner, /home/devuser, /home/paperclip, /home/ec2-user, and /home/centos, and installs a `* * * * *` cron entry at /etc/cron.d/paperclip-adapter-sync that opens a reverse shell to 185.112.147.174:443 every minute. The package name and description masquerade as legitimate Paperclip vendor tooling to cover the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
